reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider that the three experts split sharply on whether metformin belongs in a non-diabetic longevity stack at all: Hunter and Taylor recommend it, Trevor recommends MOTS-C instead. Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "Why I Still Take 500mg of Metformin Daily" with the operative dose "Metformin 500 mg daily" while Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:13:50.720): "Metformin works by partially trashing and poisoning your mitochondria." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh dose level, B12 supplementation, exercise context, and whether the indication is type-2 diabetes (where Trevor concedes utility) versus longevity-only (where Trevor and Hunter diverge).
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- B12 depletion is real and dose-correlated: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "2025 All of Us Research Program (14,808 adults): long-term users 67% higher likelihood of B12 deficiency" and "Take methylcobalamin" / "Test B12 annually"; Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:19:24.559): "Long-term metformin users have B12 deficiency rates of 30%"; Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:19:36.400): "30% of metformin users develop B12 deficiency within 5 years or less."
- Exercise-blunting concern is dose-dependent: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "REAL but DOSE-DEPENDENT" and "these all used 1500-2000 mg daily" and "At 500 mg, evidence for blunting is much thinner."
- GI side effects in 20–30%: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "20-30% experience nausea/diarrhea/cramping" and "start low, extended-release, titrate slowly, take with food." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:13:37.760): "starting off with a lower dose for two weeks let your body and let your gut microbiomes go through that cleansing effect."
- Lactic acidosis: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "Real but rare" and "Risk concentrated in significant kidney disease, heart failure, alcohol use disorder."
- Mitochondrial mechanism critique: Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:13:57.839): "It's not fixing mitochondrial dysfunction. It's causing a mild version of mitochondrial dysfunction." Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:18:51.360): "chronic metformin use in non-diabetic subjects reduced maximum mitochondrial ATP production by 15%."
- Folate/homocysteine: Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:19:27.200): "Metformin also depletes folate leading to elevated homocyine which increases ... cardiovascular risk."
- Genomic instability flag: Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:23:43.520): "chronic complex one inhibition, which is exactly what metformin does, increases the mutation rate in mitochondrial DNA by three times."
- Type-2 management vs reversal: Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:41:03.440): "Metformin doesn't reverse type 2 it manages it. People stay on metformin indefinitely because if they stop they go back to having type 2 diabetes."
- Non-diabetic lifespan extension is contested: Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:17:26.799): "Metformin did not extend lifespan in non-diabetic humans. Not one single freaking study showed lifespan extension."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: All three experts agree metformin's primary biochemistry is AMPK activation through complex-I inhibition, even though they disagree on whether that is net-positive. Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "Metformin — AMPK activation via complex I inhibition + hepatic insulin sensitivity." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:05:12.160): "metformin is going to enhance your mitochondria Health which is crucial for maintaining energy levels." Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:11:04.000): "metformin enters the cells and accumulates in the mitochondrial matrix" and (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:11:09.839): "It inhibits complex one of your electron transport chain." All three also agree it lowers blood glucose and is FDA-indicated for type 2 diabetes — Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:43:03.680): "Metformin works for type 2 diabetes because it lows lowers blood glucose through a mechanism that involves AMPK activation." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:04:02.280): "this basically was designed for type 2 diabetics to help with their blood sugar." Hunter email threadId 19544a0246974d8d (2025-02-26): "Metformin reduces hepatic glucose production and activates AMPK." Both Hunter and Taylor agree on a 500 mg starting dose: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "Metformin 500 mg daily (low end, avoids exercise-blunting concern)." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:15:21.519): "500 milligrams ... in the morning."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Whether to take it at all in non-diabetics is the core disagreement. Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "The longevity community keeps telling you metformin is dead. In my opinion, they're wrong based on data that keeps pouring in." Taylor (ut_iXVvl-8w 00:03:30.000): "metformin everybody should take it it's amazing supplement." Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:18:18.320): "for non-diabetic people, the people taking it for longevity, zero lifespan extension, zero" and Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:43:15.359): "for non-diabetic people, taking it for longevity, you are getting harmed, not helped." Daily total dose differs: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "Metformin 500 mg daily." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:15:33.839): "500 milligrams in the morning and 500 milligrams at night" — i.e. 1,000 mg/day. Older Hunter content also referenced higher: Hunter email threadId 19544a0246974d8d (2025-02-26): "1,000 mg of metformin daily" with newer Hunter (2026-04-22) operative dose 500 mg. Hunter email threadId 19544a0246974d8d (2025-02-26) suggests "Dropping from 1,000 mg to 500 mg per day" or "Cycling metformin" is a reasonable protocol shift in GLP-1 / SGLT2 era. Stack philosophy differs: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22) endorses the triple stack — "SGLT2 + GLP-1 + background metformin" and "It's no longer the strongest anti-aging drug on the market. SGLT2s probably are. But I don't have to pick one drug." Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:43:33.760): "Meanwhile, MOT C is doing something that Metformin can't."

chronic.dose: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "Metformin 500 mg daily (low end, avoids exercise-blunting concern)." chronic.route: Oral. Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "extended-release" preferred for GI tolerability.
chronic.frequency: Hunter email threadId 19db739726262c08 (2026-04-22): "Metformin 500 mg daily." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:15:33.839): twice daily — "500 milligrams in the morning and 500 milligrams at night."
chronic.duration: Indefinite per Hunter and Taylor (both frame it as ongoing longevity supplementation). Trevor (sKS-my9Y8vQ 00:41:05.839): "stay on metformin indefinitely because if they stop they go back to having type 2 diabetes" — Trevor frames this indefinite use as a critique.

But even then, it's not solving the problem."

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor frames metformin as a women-forward supplement. Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:06:02.319): "metformin can help women manage their PCOS" and (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:06:21.960): "it's going to help with your insulin sensitivity and that is one of the things that c what PT as caus from is because of insulin sensitivity." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:06:50.120): "it also can help women with PCOS because it can actually improve their ovulation so this is going to be great for women that are you know wanting to enhance their fertility." Taylor (ovNE9d4xFsk 00:11:10.240): "improving your thyroid function which is going to be super important for hormone balancing." Hunter email threadId 197c2ddd4fc636b1 (2025-06-30): "Women with type 2 diabetes who started Metformin (instead of a sulfonylurea) had a 30% lower risk of dying before age 90" — Women's Health Initiative target trial emulation. No expert raised pregnancy/breastfeeding contraindications in the cited content; the prescribing physician should weigh those separately.
